choices.name: chcCreateBaseline
choices.choiceText: Create first Baseline for future Phase

choices.name: chcEditPhase_wo_Baseline
choices.choiceText: Edit existing Phase with no Baseline

choices.name: chcReviseAwardBaseline
choices.choiceText: Revise Baseline for total Award Duration

choices.name: chcRevisePhaseBaseline
choices.choiceText: Revise Baseline for an active Phase

choices.name: Iconfirm
choices.choiceText: I confirm my properties are aligned with the above guidance.

decisions.name: Are_there_original_baseline_measure_details_records
decisions.label: Are there original baseline measure details records?

decisions.name: Is_Phase_Request_Linked_to_a_PCR
decisions.label: Is Phase Request Linked to a PCR?

decisions.name: Phase_Requests_found_that_require_amendments
decisions.label: Phase Requests found that require amendments?

Flow.description: Phase Request screen flow on the Warm Homes Portal only for Challenge Funds. GR's use this screen flow to amend any previously submitted Phase Requests that require changes (requested by the PSO). Once submitted, a new Change Control Request record is created, with the previous version being set to historic.
Flow.label: GMS - Phase Request Feedback Form

loops.name: Loop_through_Grant_Drawdowns_from_Repeater
loops.label: Loop through Grant Drawdowns from Repeater

recordDeletes.name: Delete_Original_Baseline_Measure_Detail_Records
recordDeletes.label: Delete Original Baseline Measure Detail Records

recordLookups.description: Gets all the active Phase Baselines from the Funding Award.
recordLookups.name: Get_all_Phase_Baselines
recordLookups.label: Get all Phase Baselines

recordLookups.description: Get's all Phase Requests with a Status of "Awaiting Changes (GR)".
recordLookups.name: Get_Phase_Requests_Requiring_Amendments
recordLookups.label: Get Phase Requests Requiring Amendments

fields.name: Copy_1_of_PhaseRequestFormDisplayText
fields.fieldText:
Phase Request Form Update

﻿{!PhaseRequestsRequiringChanges.firstSelectedRow.Time_Period__r.Name}

fields.name: infodisplaytext
fields.fieldText:
Declarations


You should note that if, for extraordinary circumstances, if you select 'No' for any of the following statements, reasoning should be provided for doing so. Phase requests omitting reasoning for the selection of 'no' will automatically be rejected.

fields.name: I_confirm_that_all_homes_included_in_this_phase_are_suitable_for_retrofit_KPI1
fields.fieldText: I. I confirm that all homes included in this phase (across all consortium members) are suitable for retrofit (KPI 1), having completed retrofit assessments and a confirmed EPC rating.

fields.name: Please_include_a_justification_of_why_this_is_the_case_as_well_as_the_true_of_re
fields.fieldText: If "No" is selected, please provide: A clear justification for this response & The actual % of homes with completed retrofit assessments, and Where completion is below 100%, where completion is below 100%, please provide a clear explanation of the reasons why and a clear timeline for when you expect to reach 100%

fields.name: I_confirm_that_all_homes_in_this_phase_are_ready_for_installations_to_star
fields.fieldText: II. I confirm that all homes included in this phase (across all consortium members) are ready for installations to commence (KPI 2), with 100% resident sign-ups secured.

fields.name: Please_include_a_justification_of_why_this_is_the_case_as_well_as_the_true_of_co
fields.fieldText: If "No" is selected, please provide: A clear justification for this response, and The actual % of resident sign-ups achieved Where sign-ups are below 100%, where completion is below 100%, please provide a clear explanation of the reasons why and a clear timeline for when you expect to reach 100%

fields.name: This_phase_request_matches_my_Phase_Request_baseline_previously_submitted_and_ou
fields.fieldText: III. This phase request matches my Phase Request baseline previously submitted and outlined in the GFA.

fields.name: Please_include_a_justification_of_why_this_phase_request_does_not_match_your_pre
fields.fieldText: Please include a justification of why this phase request does not match your previously submitted baseline.

fields.name: I_am_able_to_carry_over_any_deviation_to_my_Phase_Request_baseline_into_other_ph
fields.fieldText: IV. If this phase results in more than 15% of the grant funding and/or total project funding being spent on A&A costs, I confirm that I will still be able to comply with the scheme's A&A policy across the overall project.

fields.name: Please_include_a_justification_of_why_you_cannot_carry_over_any_deviation
fields.fieldText: Please include a justification of why you cannot carry over any deviation.

fields.name: I_acknowledge_that_this_phase_request_will_result_in_a_change_and_I_confirm_that
fields.fieldText: V. I acknowledge that this phase request will result in a change and I confirm that I have attached an accompanying Project Change Request.

fields.name: If_the_phase_will_see_greater_than_15_of_grant_funding_and_or_15_total_funding_s
fields.fieldText: VI. If the phase will see greater than 15% of grant funding and/or 15% total funding spend on A&A costs, I confirm I will still be able to comply with the scheme's A&A policy for the overall project.

fields.name: Please_include_a_justification_of_why_you_won_t_be_able_to_comply_with_the_schem
fields.fieldText: Please include a justification of why you won't be able to comply with the scheme's A&A policy.

fields.name: grantrecipientdcatext
fields.fieldText:
Phase Request Form

{!PhaseRequestsRequiringChanges.firstSelectedRow.Time_Period__r.Name}

fields.name: pleaseprovideDCARAG
fields.fieldText:
Grant Recipient Phase Delivery Confidence Assessment (DCA)


Please provide your Delivery Confidence Assessment for this phase, choosing one of the following RAG ratings:


Green - Minimal/No risk and highly confident scope of works will be completed within the stipulated time, cost and quality parameters.

Amber/Green - Some risk and fairly confident that the scope of works will be completed within the stipulated time, cost and quality parameters.

Amber - Higher risk and partially confident that the scope of works will be completed within the stipulated time, cost and quality parameters.

Red - Significantly high risk and very low confidence that the scope of works will be completed within the stipulated time, cost and quality parameters.

fields.name: Grant_Recipient_Phase_Delivery_Confidence_Assessment_DCA
fields.fieldText: Grant Recipient Phase Delivery Confidence Assessment (DCA)

fields.name: Copy_2_of_Measurestext
fields.fieldText:
Phase Request Form

{!PhaseRequestsRequiringChanges.firstSelectedRow.Time_Period__r.Name}

fields.name: Is_this_Phase_Request_linked_to_a_PCR
fields.fieldText: Is this Phase Request linked to a PCR?

fields.name: Measurestext
fields.fieldText:
Phase Request Form

{!PhaseRequestsRequiringChanges.firstSelectedRow.Time_Period__r.Name}

fields.name: Measuresdisplaytext
fields.fieldText:
Measure types to be installed within this phase


Please refer to the Scheme Guidance to ensure measures meet scheme eligibility (section 2.9, p.23).


The figures entered into the 'Cost to install measure' field should be best estimate averages, and the costs listed for this question are for information only, rather than forming part of the detailed baseline for this phase of the project.


Scheme Guidance: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/warm-homes-social-housing-fund-wave-3

fields.name: Cost_to_install_measure
fields.fieldText: Cost to install per this measure

fields.name: Copy_1_of_guidancetext
fields.fieldText: You don't currently have any existing Phase Requests that require amendments.

fields.name: phasehomeandcoststext
fields.fieldText:
Phase Request Form

{!PhaseRequestsRequiringChanges.firstSelectedRow.Time_Period__r.Name}

fields.name: KPIstext
fields.fieldText:
KPIs and phase homes and costs


  • All costs recorded must apply to this phase of the project only, not the full project. Where no phase costs are incurred in a given financial year, a value of 0 must be entered in the fields.
  • Please refer to your grant and co‑funding allocations for each financial year before entering costs. This is to ensure that costs entered do not exceed the allocated grant funding for each financial year. You can view this information by navigating to the Home page and reviewing the Key Data table.
  • The example is provided for illustrative purposes only. Grant Recipients should apply judgement based on their individual project timelines and spend profiles. For example, if your phase starts and ends within FY2, you should only enter costs for the 2026/27 financial year below. If your phase starts in 2026/27 and ends in 2027/28, and you expect to incur costs in both years, please apportion costs across the 2026/27 and 2027/28 financial years accordingly.
  • Overall co‑funding must be a minimum of 50% of eligible project costs, excluding costs for homes accessing the on‑gas grid low‑carbon heat incentive offer. Any co‑funding for these homes will not count towards the 50% requirement.
  • Costs entered are the maximum forecast spend for this phase and will be baselined. Any increase in phase costs post‑approval will require a Project Change Request (PCR).
  • Please note that the estimated costs listed for LCH homes are provided for information purposes only and do not form part of the detailed baseline for this phase of the project.

fields.name: Total_number_of_homes_to_be_upgraded_in_phase
fields.fieldText: Total number of homes to be upgraded in phase

fields.name: No_of_homes_accessing_the_off_gas_grid_LCH_cost_cap_uplift_in_phase
fields.fieldText: No. of homes accessing the off-gas grid LCH cost cap uplift in phase

fields.name: No_of_homes_accessing_the_on_gas_grid_LCH_incentive_offer_in_phase
fields.fieldText: No. of homes accessing the on-gas grid LCH incentive offer in phase

fields.fieldText: Co-funded capital costs on off-gas LCH homes

fields.fieldText: Total phase costs for this FY

fields.name: Maximum_grant_funded_capital_costs_off_gas_LCH_homes2627
fields.fieldText: Grant funded capital costs on off-gas LCH homes

fields.name: Maximum_co_funded_capital_costs_off_gas_LCH_homes2627
fields.fieldText: Co-funded capital costs on off-gas LCH homes

fields.name: Maximum_grant_funded_capital_costs_on_gas_LCH_homes2627

fields.name: Maximum_total_phase_costs3
fields.fieldText: Total phase costs for this FY

fields.name: Maximum_grant_funded_capital_costs_off_gas_LCH_homes2728
fields.fieldText: Grant funded capital costs on off-gas LCH homes

fields.name: Maximum_co_funded_capital_costs_off_gas_LCH_homes2728
fields.fieldText: Co-funded capital costs on off-gas LCH homes

fields.name: Maximum_grant_funded_capital_costs_on_gas_LCH_homes2728
fields.fieldText: Grant funded capital costs on on-gas LCH homes

fields.name: Maximum_total_phase_costs
fields.fieldText: Total phase costs for this FY

fields.name: Co_funded_capital_costs_on_off_gas_LCH_homes2829
fields.fieldText: Co-funded capital costs on off-gas LCH homes

fields.name: Co_funded_capital_costs_on_off_gas_LCH_homes2930
fields.fieldText: Co-funded capital costs on off-gas LCH homes

fields.name: phaserequesttext
fields.fieldText:
Phase Request Form

{!PhaseRequestsRequiringChanges.firstSelectedRow.Time_Period__r.Name}

fields.name: beginphasetext
fields.fieldText:
Type and condition of homes to be upgraded in this phase


In order to begin a phase, 100% of retrofit assessments should be complete, unless in exceptional circumstances. Please complete the below tables with all starting and ending EPCs of all homes in scope within this phase.


If infill homes are included in this phase, please provide a justification as to why they are permissible, as per section 2.4 of the Scheme Guidance.


As a reminder:


Infill: Homes starting at EPC C+ cannot exceed 10% of the total number of project homes. Non-social homes cannot exceed 30% of the total number of project homes. If this phase contains more than 10% EPC C+ or 30% non-social homes, then you must explain in the justification section how this will be compensated for in later phases.


All homes are expected to reach EPC C, but we accept that there may be circumstances for homes at EPC bands E-G where this is not achievable within the cost caps. If this is the case, homes are expected to attain the maximum EPC grade achievable within the cost caps, and a corresponding justification should be provided below.


DESNZ encourages applicants to avoid project homes ending above EPC C, to the extent that is possible. A GR must provide assurances that for any non-infill homes with ending EPCs of B or A, no grant funding is being used for additional measures after an EPC of C has been achieved. Furthermore, any spend on measures after a property has already reached EPC C cannot be included as part of your co-funding figures. This does not apply to infill homes starting at EPC C+. 


See the full Scheme Guidance for further information (section 2.4, p.17)


The same homes should not feature in more than one phase/phase request.

fields.name: Starting_EPC
fields.fieldText: Starting EPC - Number of Homes at each EPC grade

fields.name: Ending_EPC
fields.fieldText: Ending EPC - Number of Homes at each EPC grade

fields.name: Number_of_non_social_homes_included_in_this_phase
fields.fieldText: Number of non-social homes included in this phase

fields.name: If_you_have_included_any_homes_that_either_start_at_EPC_C_or_any_ending_below_EP
fields.fieldText: If you have included any homes that either start at EPC C+ or any ending below EPC C, and/or any non-social homes, please provide a justification as to why they are permissible, as per the policies outlined in the Scheme Guidance. If you have included any non-social homes, please also confirm that these do not exceed the maximum limit of 70% of homes within a single block/terrace. If you have listed any non-infill homes with ending EPCs of B or A, please provide assurances that no grant funding is being used for additional measures after an EPC of C has been achieved. Please also confirm that any spend on measures after a property has already reached EPC C is not being included as part of your co-funding figures.

fields.name: By_ticking_here_you_confirm_that_your_properties_achieving_EPC_A_or_B_are_aligne
fields.fieldText: By ticking here you confirm that your properties achieving EPC A or B are aligned with the above guidance. Please note that we may request evidence of this, including in the form of retrofit assessments, RdSAP modelling and cost data. Please note that DESNZ’s approval of this Phase Request is not confirmation that we have reviewed and confirmed the evidence. We reserve the right to request and review this evidence retrospectively after a Phase Request is approved.

fields.name: spendandgrantdrawdowntext
fields.fieldText:
Phase Request Form

{!PhaseRequestsRequiringChanges.firstSelectedRow.Time_Period__r.Name}

fields.name: forecasttext
fields.fieldText:
Please complete the Forecast of grant drawdown requests table to indicate the £ amount and month of all drawdown requests to be submitted for this phase (even if not taking place during this phase).


Please be reminded that:


While this response is for information only and will not form part of your formal baseline, all figures must align with your baselined financial year spend breakdown.


﻿Grant spend in a financial year must be paid out in the same financial year. The final drawdown request for spend incurred in the same financial year must be submitted in January.

fields.name: guidancetext
fields.fieldText:
Feedback on submitted Phase Request


{!PhaseRequestsRequiringChanges.firstSelectedRow.PSO_Feedback__c}

fields.name: Copy_1_of_Measurestext
fields.fieldText:
Phase Request Form

{!PhaseRequestsRequiringChanges.firstSelectedRow.Time_Period__r.Name}

fields.name: Thankyoutext
fields.fieldText:
Thank you for your submission.

 

The Wave 3 Delivery Partner will endeavour to assess the Phase Request (PR) as promptly as possible and you will be contacted by a DSM via email after no later than 7 working days to confirm whether the PR has been approved with a Green rating, or escalated to DESNZ for further review.

 

If escalated, the turnaround time for a final decision will depend on the initial rating given to the PR:

•    Amber/Green or Amber – Up to an additional 5 working days (12 working days total)

•    Red – Up to an additional 10 working days (17 working days total)


If this submission is just an initial draft for your DSM to provide their feedback on, please ensure you have clearly communicated this to them.

 

Otherwise, the DSM will undertake initial completion and validation checks, and contact you immediately via email if any issues are identified.

 

NB: If this PR submission is accompanied by a Project Change Request (PCR), the PCR must be approved before the PR can be. In this scenario, the turnaround time for a PR final decision is up to 25 working days from the first day of the month, if submitted alongside a PCR before the end of the preceding month. A DSM will contact the Grant Recipient shortly after receiving a PCR to provide an estimated turnaround time for a final decision on the PCR.

 

If you have any questions about the PR assessment and approval process, please contact your DSM.
